context: Evaluates whether the candidate correctly implements an end-to-end CVE remediation orchestration skill following the rh-sre remediation skill pattern. The skill must include upfront task planning, sequential sub-skill invocation, a remediatable gate, and two mandatory HITL checkpoints.

checklist.name: Upfront planned task list with approval gate
checklist.description: Before any sub-skill invocation, the skill presents all planned tasks in workflow execution order and waits for explicit user approval ('yes' or 'proceed'). No sub-skill is invoked until this approval is received.

checklist.name: All 7 sub-skills referenced
checklist.description: The workflow references all seven required sub-skill categories: (1) MCP prerequisite validation, (2) CVE impact analysis, (3) CVE validation, (4) system context gathering, (5) playbook generation, (6) playbook execution, and (7) remediation verification. All are invoked using the /skill-name slash format.

checklist.name: Remediatable gate implemented
checklist.description: After the CVE validation step, the skill checks the remediation availability flag. If not remediatable, it explains why, suggests manual alternatives, and asks 'Continue anyway? (yes/no)' before proceeding. It does NOT silently continue or hard-stop.

checklist.name: Remediation plan before execution
checklist.description: Before invoking the playbook execution sub-skill, the skill presents a remediation plan containing: a 1-2 sentence summary, a table (CVE | Target Systems | Key Action), and an ordered checklist. It waits for confirmation ('yes'/'proceed'/'dry-run only'/'abort') and does not execute until confirmed.

checklist.name: Sequential execution enforcement
checklist.description: The workflow explicitly states that each step must complete before the next begins. The skill uses 'do not start Step N+1 until Step N has returned its result' or equivalent language to enforce sequential execution.
